trigger: User says 'something keeps killing this workspace' or sees frequent session_id changes for the same cmux workspace
rule: The cmux workspace is NOT being killed — the claude PROCESS inside it is exiting, and cmux is auto-relaunching it from a captured launchCommand stored in ~/Library/Application Support/cmux/session-com.cmuxterm.app.json. Each relaunch creates a new session_id and a new ~/.claude/projects/<cwd-slug>/<id>.jsonl, which makes it look like the workspace was killed. Investigate the claude exit cause: (a) /clear-and-resume skill — intentional, can be replaced with manual /clear; (b) Bedrock token expiry — refresh STS before timeout; (c) context-window auto-compact + exit — Claude Code may exit on certain compact paths; (d) node uncaught exception inside claude. Check ~/.claude/debug/<session-uuid>.txt for the exiting session for stack traces.
why: On 2026-05-22 Mukul saw 4 different claude sessions for the Assistant workspace in 88 minutes (017b08cf, c9249027, ad4ba93d, 36408d13). cmux session-com.cmuxterm.app.json showed launchCommand{source:environment} per workspace — cmux captures and replays the claude command on exit. workspace:97 alive throughout, login shell PID 93944 + zsh PID 93945 stable since Thursday 6 PM. The workspace is NEVER being killed. Same misdiagnosis would have applied if I'd believed the dashboard 'live sessions' count without inspecting cmux internals.

sources.user_correction: On 2026-05-22 Mukul saw 4 different claude sessions for the Assistant workspace in 88 minutes (017b08cf, c9249027, ad4ba93d, 36408d13). cmux session-com.cmuxterm.app.json showed launchCommand{source:environment} per workspace — cmux captures and replays the claude command on exit. workspace:97 alive throughout, login shell PID 93944 + zsh PID 93945 stable since Thursday 6 PM. The workspace is NEVER being killed. Same misdiagnosis would have applied if I'd believed the dashboard 'live sessions' count without inspecting cmux internals.
